raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Transgender, nonbinary, and other LGBTQ+ youth are at risk due to a growing trend of forced outing policies in California schools.",
    "More than a dozen California school districts have proposed and/or passed policies requiring teachers to notify parents if their child identifies as transgender or asks to be identified by a different name or pronouns.",
    "Forced outing policies have a measurable impact on the mental health of LGBTQ+ students.",
    "Forced outing policies can lead to a rise in bullying, harassment, and discrimination against LGBTQ+ students.",
    "Since 2020, six states have passed laws requiring school staff to forcibly out transgender students.",
    "California would be the first state to explicitly prohibit forced outing policies in schools.",
    "Less than 40% of transgender and nonbinary youth report that their homes are LGBTQ-affirming, according to a 2024 Trevor Project survey.",
    "More than half of transgender and nonbinary young people found their school to be gender-affirming.",
    "Those who attend gender-affirming schools report lower rates of attempting suicide.",
    "The SAFETY Act will prohibit school districts from implementing forced outing policies.",
    "The SAFETY Act will provide resources for parents and students to navigate conversations around gender and identity.",
    "The SAFETY Act will ensure teachers or school staff are not retaliated against for refusing to forcibly out a student.",
    "Forced outing policies violate state and federal anti-discrimination and privacy laws."
  ]
}
```
